executive_questions_answered: How far off plan could we be in a downside scenario, and do we have a plan for it?
What upside could we capture if things go better than expected?
How does our base case compare to best- and worst-case outcomes?

metric_definitions.definition: Difference between ARR in a given scenario (upside or downside) and the base case ARR. Quantifies revenue gap under alternate assumptions.

known_variants.definition_delta: Expresses scenario ARR delta as a percentage of base case ARR
known_variants.when_to_use: Communicating magnitude of upside/downside in relative terms

pitfalls: Not all scenarios are equally likely; avoid treating upside/downside as forecasts
Single-factor sensitivity may overlook combined effects of multiple variables
Scenarios rely on assumption quality – unrealistic inputs yield useless scenarios

metric_definitions.definition: Revenue impact of a small change (e.g., ±1%) in a key assumption (churn rate, sales productivity, etc.), identifying which inputs most influence outcomes.

known_variants.definition_delta: Measures % change in ARR for 1% change in input (normalized sensitivity)
known_variants.when_to_use: Comparing sensitivity across metrics or businesses

pitfalls: Assumes linear response – real systems may have nonlinear thresholds
Interdependencies: Changing one assumption can affect others, not isolated
Over-focusing on one sensitive assumption might neglect other risks

joins_and_keys.notes: Assumptions applied in model; not joined to transactional data directly

transformation_rules: Ensure scenario inputs are internally consistent (e.g., if churn increases in downside, NRR should drop accordingly)
Use baseline actual metrics as starting point for scenarios
Exclude extraordinary one-off events from baseline to avoid skewing scenario projections

data_quality_checks.logic: Verify Upside >= Base >= Downside for all key metrics (monotonic scenario outcomes)
Flag if downside scenario yields negative or near-zero ARR (overly pessimistic assumptions)

aggregation_rules: Each scenario is calculated independently; no aggregation except to portfolio-level totals
If segmented (e.g., by region), sum scenario outputs for company-wide view
calculations_and_models.forecast_logic: Base case aligns with official forecast. Upside/downside adjust key drivers (growth rates, churn, costs) to model best/worst outcomes.

layout.purpose: Compare revenue and growth outcomes under base, upside, and downside scenarios
Show which assumptions have the largest impact on outcomes

narrative_insights_to_generate: Identify which variables drive the biggest upside vs downside differences
Indicate whether the base case skews conservative or aggressive relative to scenarios
diagnostic_questions: What assumptions would need to come true to achieve the upside scenario?
If trends start matching downside scenario assumptions, how much would our plan miss by?

recommended_actions.action: Set trigger conditions to implement cost controls (downside preparedness)
recommended_actions.trigger_condition: KPIs begin tracking closer to downside scenario values

recommended_actions.action: Outline investment opportunities (hiring, marketing) to seize upside
recommended_actions.trigger_condition: Early performance exceeds base and aligns with upside scenario

implementation_notes.minimum_viable_version: Simple scenario toggle changing a few high-level metrics (e.g., ±X% sales, ±Y% churn)
implementation_notes.advanced_version: Comprehensive scenario planning tool linking all key inputs (sales capacity, marketing spend, churn drivers) with probabilistic outcomes
common_client_customizations: More granular scenario tiers (e.g., mild vs severe downside)
Integrated scenario into financial statements for P&L impact
privacy_and_access_notes: Scenarios are exploratory – label clearly to avoid confusion with committed forecasts
Limit detailed assumption access to planning teams; share summarized impacts more broadly
success_criteria: Organization agrees on clear action plans tied to each scenario
No major surprise outside of scenario ranges (actual performance falls within modeled bounds)
